name: ip igmp snooping
detail: Globally enable Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) snooping on the device or to enable it on a per-VLAN basis.
usageSummary: When IGMP snooping is enabled globally, it is enabled in all of the existing VLAN interfaces.  When IGMP snooping is globally disabled, it is disabled on all of the existing VLAN interfaces.  VLAN IDs 1002 to 1005 are reserved for Token Ring and FDDI VLANs, and cannot be used in IGMP snooping.

name: ip igmp snooping last-member-query-count
detail: Configure how often Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) snooping will send query messages in response to receiving an IGMP leave message.
syntax: ip igmp snooping [vlan vlan-id] last-member-query-count count
noForm: no ip igmp snooping [vlan vlan-id] last-member-query-count count

params.name: count
params.description: Interval at which query messages are sent, in milliseconds. The range is from 1―7. The default is 2.

usageSummary: When a multicast host leaves a group, the host sends an IGMP leave message.  To check if this host is the last to leave the group, IGMP query messages are sent when the leave message is seen until the last-member-query-interval timeout period expires.  If no response is received to the last-member queries before the timeout period expires, the group record is deleted.

name: ip igmp snooping last-member-query-interval
detail: Enable the Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) configurable-leave timer globally or on a per-VLAN basis.
syntax: ip igmp snooping [vlan vlan-id] last-member-query-interval time
noForm: no ip igmp snooping [vlan vlan-id] last-member-query-interval time

params.name: time
params.description: Interval time out in seconds. The range is 100 to 32767 milliseconds.

usageSummary: When IGMP snooping is globally enabled, IGMP snooping is enabled on all the existing VLAN interfaces.  When IGMP snooping is globally disabled, IGMP snooping is disabled on all the existing VLAN interfaces.  VLAN IDs 1002 to 1005 are reserved for Token Ring and FDDI VLANs and cannot be used in IGMP snooping.

name: ip igmp snooping querier
detail: Globally enable the Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) querier function in Layer 2 networks.
syntax: ip igmp snooping [vlan vlan-id] querier [address ip-address | max-response-time response-time | query-interval interval-count | tcn query {count count | interval interval} | timer expiry expiry-time | version version] tcn query {count | interval} | timer expiry | version]
noForm: no ip igmp snooping [vlan vlan-id] querier [address | max-response-time | query-interval |

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) Enables IGMP snooping and the IGMP querier function on the specified VLAN. Ranges are 1—1001 and 1006—4094.

params.name: address ip-address
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a source IP address. If you do not specify an IP address, the querier tries to use the global IP address configured for the IGMP querier.

params.name: max-response-time
params.description: (Optional) Sets the maximum time to wait for an IGMP querier report.

params.name: response-time
params.description: The range is 1—25 seconds.

params.name: query-interval interval-count
params.description: (Optional) Sets the interval between IGMP queriers. The range is 1—18000 seconds.

params.name: tcn query
params.description: (Optional) Sets parameters related to Topology Change Notifications (TCNs).

params.name: count count
params.description: Sets the number of TCN queries to be executed during the TCN interval time. The range is 1—10.

params.name: interval interval
params.description: Sets the TCN query interval time. The range is 1—255.

params.name: timer expiry expiry-time
params.description: (Optional) Sets the length of time until the IGMP querier expires. The range is 60—300 seconds.

params.name: version version
params.description: (Optional) Selects the IGMP version number that the querier feature uses. Select either 1 or 2.

usageSummary: Use this command to enable IGMP snooping to detect the IGMP version and IP address of a device that sends IGMP query messages, which is also called a querier.  By default, the IGMP snooping querier is configured to detect devices that use IGMP Version 2 (IGMPv2), but does not detect clients that are using IGMP Version 1 (IGMPv1).  You can manually configure the max-response-time value when devices use IGMPv2.

name: ip igmp snooping report-suppression
detail: Enable Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) report suppression.
usageSummary: IGMP report suppression is supported only when the multicast query has IGMPv1 and IGMPv2 reports.  This feature is not supported when the query includes IGMPv3 reports.  The device uses IGMP report suppression to forward only one IGMP report per multicast router query to multicast devices.

name: ip igmp snooping robustness-variable
detail: Configure the IGMP robustness variable globally or on a per-VLAN basis.
syntax: ip igmp snooping [vlan vlan-id] robustness-variable number
noForm: no ip igmp snooping [vlan vlan-id] robustness-variable number

params.name: number
params.description: Robustness variable number. The range is 1 to 3.

name: ip igmp snooping vlan immediate-leave
syntax: ip igmp snooping vlan vlan-id immediate-leave
noForm: no ip igmp snooping vlan vlan-id immediate-leave

params.name: vlan-id
params.description: Enables IGMPv2 immediate leave processing in the specified VLAN. The range is 1 to 1001 and 1006 to 4094.

usageSummary: You can verify your settings by entering the show ip igmp snooping privileged EXEC command.  Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.

name: ip igmp snooping vlan mrouter
detail: Add a multicast router port or to configure the multicast learning method.
syntax: ip igmp snooping vlan vlan-id mrouter {interface interface-id | learn {cgmp | pim-dvmrp} } }
noForm: no ip igmp snooping vlan vlan-id mrouter {interface interface-id | learn {cgmp | pim-dvmrp}

params.name: vlan-id
params.description: Enables IGMP snooping and adds the port in the specified VLAN as the multicast router port. Ranges are 1—1001 and 1006—4094. interface interface-id Specifies the next-hop interface to the multicast router. The interface-id value has these options: • fastethernet interface number—A Fast Ethernet IEEE 802.3 interface. • gigabitethernet interface number—A Gigabit Ethernet IEEE 802.3z interface. • tengigabitethernet interface number—A 10-Gigabit Ethernet IEEE 802.3z interface. • port-channel interface number—A channel interface. The range is 0—48.

params.name: learn
params.description: Specifies the multicast router learning method.

params.name: cgmp
params.description: Sets the switch to learn multicast router ports by snooping on Cisco Group Management Protocol (CGMP) packets.

params.name: pim-dvmrp
params.description: Sets the switch to learn multicast router ports by snooping on IGMP queries and Protocol-Independent Multicast-Distance Vector Multicast Routing Protocol (PIM-DVMRP) packets.

usageSummary: VLAN IDs 1002―1005 are reserved for Token Ring and FDDI VLANs, and cannot be used in IGMP snooping.  The CGMP learn method is useful for reducing control traffic.  The configuration is saved in NVRAM.

name: ip igmp snooping vlan static
detail: Enable Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) snooping and to statically add a Layer 2 port as a member of a multicast group.
syntax: ip igmp snooping vlan vlan-id static ip-address interface interface-id
noForm: no ip igmp snooping vlan vlan-id static ip-address interface interface-id

params.name: vlan-id
params.description: Enables IGMP snooping on the specified VLAN. Ranges are 1—1001 and 1006—4094.

params.name: ip-address
params.description: Adds a Layer 2 port as a member of a multicast group with the specified group IP address. interface interface-id Specifies the interface of the member port. The interface-id has these options: • fastethernet interface number—A Fast Ethernet IEEE 802.3 interface. • gigabitethernet interface number—A Gigabit Ethernet IEEE 802.3z interface. • tengigabitethernet interface number—A 10-Gigabit Ethernet IEEE 802.3z interface. • port-channel interface number—A channel interface. The range is 0—128.

usageSummary: VLAN IDs 1002 to 1005 are reserved for Token Ring and FDDI VLANs, and cannot be used in IGMP snooping.  The configuration is saved in NVRAM.

name: mvr
detail: Enable the multicast VLAN registration (MVR) feature on the switch.
syntax: mvr [group ip-address [count] | mode [compatible | dynamic] | querytime value | vlan vlan-id] vlan vlan-id]
noForm: no mvr [group ip-address [count] | mode [compatible | dynamic] | querytime value |

params.name: group ip-address
params.description: (Optional) Statically configures an MVR group IP multicast address on the switch. Use the no form of this command to remove a statically configured IP multicast address or contiguous addresses or, when no IP address is entered, to remove all statically configured MVR IP multicast addresses.

params.name: count
params.description: (Optional) Multiple contiguous MVR group addresses. The range is 1 to 256; the default is 0.

params.name: mode
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the MVR mode of operation. The default is compatible mode.

params.name: compatible
params.description: (Optional) Sets MVR mode to provide compatibility with Catalyst 2900 XL and Catalyst 3500 XL switches. This mode does not allow dynamic membership joins on source ports.

params.name: dynamic
params.description: (Optional) Sets MVR mode to allow dynamic MVR membership on source ports.

params.name: querytime value
params.description: (Optional) Sets the maximum time to wait for IGMP report memberships on a receiver port. This time applies only to receiver-port leave processing. When an IGMP query is sent from a receiver port, the switch waits for the default or configured MVR querytime for an IGMP group membership report before removing the port from multicast group membership. The value is the response time in units of tenths of a second. The range is 1 to 100; the default is 5 tenths or one-half second. Use the no form of the command to return to the default setting. Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 28 IGMP Snooping and MVR mvr (global configuration)

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the VLAN on which MVR multicast data is expected to be received. This is also the VLAN to which all the source ports belong. The range is 1 to 4094; the default is VLAN 1.

usageSummary: A maximum of 256 MVR multicast groups can be configured on a switch.  Use the command with keywords to set the MVR mode for a switch, configure the MVR IP multicast address, set the maximum time to wait for a query reply before removing a port from group membership, and to specify the MVR multicast VLAN.  Use the mvr group command to statically set up all the IP multicast addresses that will take part in MVR.

name: mvr
detail: Statically assign a port to an IP multicast VLAN and IP address.
syntax: mvr [immediate | type {receiver | source} | vlan vlan-id group [ip-address]]
noForm: no mvr [immediate | type | vlan vlan-id group [ip-address]]

params.name: immediate
params.description: (Optional) Enables the Immediate Leave feature of MVR on a port. Use the no mvr immediate command to disable the feature.

params.name: type
params.description: (Optional) Configures the port as an MVR receiver port or a source port. The default port type is neither an MVR source nor a receiver port. The no mvr type command resets the port as neither a source or a receiver port.

params.name: receiver
params.description: Configures the port as a subscriber port that can only receive multicast data. Receiver ports cannot belong to the multicast VLAN.

params.name: source
params.description: Configures the port as an uplink port that can send and receive multicast data for the configured multicast groups. All source ports on a switch belong to a single multicast VLAN.

params.name: vlan vlan-id group
params.description: (Optional) Adds the port as a static member of the multicast group with the specified VLAN ID. The no mvr vlan vlan-id group command removes a port on a VLAN from membership in an IP multicast address group.

params.name: ip-address
params.description: (Optional) Statically configures the specified MVR IP multicast group address for the specified multicast VLAN ID. This is the IP address of the multicast group that the port is joining.

modes: Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 31 IGMP Snooping and MVR mvr (interface configuration)
usageSummary: Configure a port as a source port if that port should be able to both send and receive multicast data bound for the configured multicast groups.  Multicast data is received on all ports configured as source ports.  Receiver ports cannot be trunk ports.

name: show ip igmp snooping
detail: Display the Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) snooping configuration of the device or the VLAN.
syntax: show ip igmp snooping [groups | mrouter | querier] [vlan vlan-id] [detail]

params.name: groups
params.description: (Optional) Displays the IGMP snooping multicast table.

params.name: mrouter
params.description: (Optional) Displays the IGMP snooping multicast router ports.

params.name: querier
params.description: (Optional) Displays the configuration and operation information for the IGMP querier. vlan vlan-id (Optional) Specifies a VLAN; the range is 1 to 1001 and 1006 to 4094.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays operational state information.

usageSummary: VLAN IDs 1002―1005 are reserved for Token Ring and FDDI VLANs, and cannot be used in IGMP snooping.  Expressions are case sensitive.  For example, if you enter | exclude output, the lines that contain "output" do not appear, but the lines that contain "Output" appear.

name: show ip igmp snooping groups
detail: Display the Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) snooping multicast table for the device or the multicast information.
syntax: show ip igmp snooping groups [vlan vlan-id ] [ [dynamic | user ] [count] | ip_address]

params.name: dynamic
params.description: (Optional) Displays IGMP snooping learned group information.

params.name: user
params.description: (Optional) Displays user-configured group information.

params.name: count
params.description: (Optional) Displays the total number of entries for the specified command options instead of the actual entries.

params.name: ip_address
params.description: (Optional) Characteristics of the multicast group with the specified group IP address.

usageSummary: Expressions are case sensitive.  For example, if you enter | exclude output, the lines that contain "output" do not appear, but the lines that contain "Output" appear.

name: show ip igmp snooping mrouter
detail: Display the Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) snooping dynamically learned and manually configured multicast router ports for the device or for the specified multicast VLAN.
syntax: show ip igmp snooping mrouter [vlan vlan-id]

params.name: vlan
params.description: vlan-id (Optional) Specifies a VLAN; Ranges are from 1―1001 and 1006―4094.

usageSummary: VLAN IDs 1002―1005 are reserved for Token Ring and FDDI VLANs, and cannot be used in IGMP snooping.  When multicast VLAN registration (MVR) is enabled, the show ip igmp snooping mrouter command displays MVR multicast router information and IGMP snooping information.  Expressions are case sensitive, for example, if you enter | exclude output, the lines that contain "output" do not appear, but the lines that contain "Output" appear.

name: show ip igmp snooping querier
detail: To display the configuration and operation information for the IGMP querier that is configured on a device, use the show ip igmp snooping queriercommand in user EXEC mode.
syntax: show ip igmp snooping querier [vlan vlan-id] [detail ]

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed IGMP querier information.

usageSummary: Use the show ip igmp snooping querier command to display the IGMP version and the IP address of a detected device, also called a querier, that sends IGMP query messages.  A subnet can have multiple multicast routers but only one IGMP querier.  In a subnet running IGMPv2, one of the multicast routers is elected as the querier.

name: show mvr
detail: Display the current Multicast VLAN Registration (MVR) global parameter values, including whether or not MVR is enabled, the MVR multicast VLAN, the maximum query response time, the number of multicast groups, and the MVR mode (dynamic or compatible).

name: show mvr interface
detail: Display the Multicast VLAN Registration (MVR) receiver and source ports.

params.name: interface-id
params.description: (Optional) Displays MVR type, status, and Immediate Leave setting for the interface. Valid interfaces include physical ports (including type, stack member (stacking-capable switches only) module, and port number).

params.name: members
params.description: (Optional) Displays all MVR groups to which the specified interface belongs.

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) Displays all MVR group members on this VLAN. The range is 1 to 4094.

usageSummary: If the entered port identification is a non-MVR port or a source port, the command returns an error message.  For receiver ports, it displays the port type, per port status, and Immediate-Leave setting.  If you enter the members keyword, all MVR group members on the interface appear.

name: show mvr members
detail: Display all receiver and source ports that are currently members of an IP multicast group.

params.name: ip-address
params.description: (Optional) The IP multicast address. If the address is entered, all receiver and source ports that are members of the multicast group appear. If no address is entered, all members of all Multicast VLAN Registration (MVR) groups are listed. If a group has no members, the group is listed as Inactive.

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) Displays all MVR group members on this VLAN. The range is 1 to 4094.

usageSummary: The show mvr members command applies to receiver and source ports.  For MVR-compatible mode, all source ports are members of all multicast groups.
